page: Microsoft Azure GS1 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor with 2 vCPUs and 2 physical cores operating at a base frequency of 2.0 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 40 MB of L3 cache, alongside support for AVX, AES, BMI2, and FMA instruction sets. The instance is equipped with 28.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 14.0 GB per core, and includes 57 GB of local storage.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the GS1 is positioned in the lower tier for compute-heavy tasks. Single-core and multi-core CPU tests via stress-ng and Geekbench place the instance in the bottom 10% to 25% of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth benchmarks show a weak to poor profile, with read-only large block bandwidth at 20.49 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 13.0 GB/sec. However, single-threaded gzip compression achieves an average performance tier at 23.85 GB/sec. Database operations and web serving throughput are relatively low, with static web serving measured at 2.38 GB/sec and Redis SET operations reaching 3.03 million operations per second.

Given its hardware profile, the GS1 offers a qualitative trade-off, providing a generous allocation of RAM relative to its limited processing power. This makes it suitable for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high CPU throughput, such as caching layers, small databases, or lightweight memory-bound data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure GS1 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor running at 2.0 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it features 28.0 GB of RAM, providing a substantial 14.0 GB of memory per core, alongside 57 GB of local storage. Benchmark results indicate limited computational power, with stress-ng and Geekbench CPU scores falling into the bottom 10% to 25% tier. Memory bandwidth is also modest, though single-threaded gzip compression performs at an average level of 23.85 GB/sec. The GS1 is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring high memory capacity relative to compute resources, making it a suitable fit for small databases, caching systems, and memory-bound applications with low CPU demands.
og_description: Microsoft Azure GS1 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 28.0 GB of RAM, and 57 GB of local storage, designed for memory-intensive workloads with low compute requirements.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine offering high RAM-to-vCPU density and local storage for memory-bound workloads with moderate compute needs.

Equipped with 28.0 GB of RAM, providing 14.0 GB of memory per core
Includes 57 GB of local storage for temporary data requirements
Delivers average single-threaded gzip compression performance at 23.85 GB/sec
Best suited for small databases, caching layers, and memory-bound applications
